task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium DICOM scan is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius main window shows the axial, coronal, and sagittal views of a skull scan.

task_completion.description: Verify that the segmentation threshold is set to the Bone preset (226-3071 HU).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold/segmentation panel shows 'Bone' selected from a dropdown, or the threshold range is explicitly set to Min: 226 and Max: 3071.

task_completion.description: Verify that the segmentation mask is inverted to capture the negative space.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The highlighted mask (usually colored) in the 2D slice views covers the empty space/background around and inside the skull rather than the bone itself, or an 'Invert' option is visibly checked/clicked in the mask properties.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 3D surface is generated from the inverted mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane displays a solid block-like or cylindrical structure (representing the full scan volume's bounding box) instead of a standard skull model.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 3D surface is exported as a binary STL to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' or 'Save As' dialog is open showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename 'negative_mold.stl' with the STL file type selected.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with InVesalius menus, threshold sliders, and 3D generation buttons, rather than terminal commands executing a script.

integrity.description: Verify the 3D surface was genuinely generated within the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inverted 3D surface (solid block) is clearly visible in the InVesalius 3D viewer pane prior to or during the export process.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not spoof the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using terminal commands like 'touch', 'echo', or a text editor to create a fake 'negative_mold.stl' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct output path is '/home/ga/Documents/negative_mold.stl'. The task uses DICOM series 'ct_cranium/0051'. A correct negative mold will have bounding box dimensions exceeding 400.0 mm (representing the full scan volume) and a minimum triangle count of 10000 (indicating the presence of the internal skull cavity).
